evidence: The statement estimates the size of the cofactor set {a/(a,b) : a,b in A}, where (a,b) is the gcd (Lean: a / a.gcd b).
The problem asks whether every A in Z/NZ of size >> N^{1/2} has a non-empty subset with sum congruent to 0 mod N (HasZeroSubsetSum) — a zero-sum problem.
The statement is about residues in Z/NZ (Lean ZMod N) with subset sums taken modulo N.
The statement concerns residues a_1..a_p mod a prime p whose zero-sum subsets S (sum_{i in S} a_i = 0 mod p) all have the same size — a zero-sum problem (Graham's conjecture).
Gao-Hamidoune-Wang variant: zero-sum subsequences mod arbitrary moduli p all forced to one length imply at most two distinct residues.
Erdős-Szemerédi variant for sufficiently large primes of the same zero-sum-subset statement mod p.
The docstring states the condition sum_{i in S} a_i = 0 (mod p) for residues modulo a prime p (Lean ZMod p).
The docstring asks for the largest A in {1..N} with no k+1 relatively prime elements (Lean: not Set.Pairwise Nat.Coprime).
The statement asks for the growth of R_r(n), the r-uniform hypergraph Ramsey number (Lean: hypergraphRamsey r n).
The docstring defines R_r(n) via 2-colourings of the complete r-uniform hypergraph.
The statement asks whether the 3-uniform hypergraph Ramsey number R_3(n) grows doubly exponentially (Lean: hypergraphRamsey 3 n).
The docstring defines R_3(n) via 2-colourings of the 3-uniform hypergraph on m vertices.
The statement bounds the size Ramsey number r-hat(G,H) (Lean: sizeRamsey G H) linearly in the number of edges of H.
Part (i) asks whether Q_3, the 3-dimensional hypercube graph on 8 vertices and 12 edges, is Ramsey size linear; the Wikidata item's alias 'hypercube graph Q3' names exactly this graph.
Part (ii) asks whether K_{3,3}, the complete bipartite graph with parts of size 3 (the utility graph), is Ramsey size linear.
The question is whether the size Ramsey number r-hat(Q_3, H) is O(m) for every H with m edges (IsRamseySizeLinear).
The question is whether the size Ramsey number r-hat(K_{3,3}, H) is O(m) for every H with m edges (IsRamseySizeLinear).
The question is whether the size Ramsey number r-hat(H_5, H) is O(m) for every H with m edges (IsRamseySizeLinear).
The problem asks whether every dense K_{2,2,2}-free graph must contain an independent set of size >>_delta n (Lean: G.indepNum).
The EHSS partial result gives a linear-size independent set in K_{2,2,2}-free graphs with edge density above 1/8.

The source reference [EHSS83] is 'More results on Ramsey-Turán type problems' and the problem trades forbidden-subgraph edge density against independence number, the defining question of Ramsey-Turán theory.
